video_title: Guy Goes ALL IN on a Steak Hoagie in Hollywood, FL | Diners, Drive-Ins and Dives | Food Network

restaurants.name: Sonny's Famous Steak Hokies
restaurants.guy_intro: I'm in Hollywood, not California, but Florida, where I am getting ready to enjoy a Philly cheesesteak and not seafood at a place called Sonny's that's owned by a guy named John at a building that looks new but is actually 50 years old. Oh, I know, this makes complete sense. We gotta check out Sonny's Famous Steak Hokies.

dishes.description: Rolls made daily from scratch with water, yeast, salt, dye malt, flour, butter, and shortening. Mixed, proofed, punched down, rolled, scored, buttered, and baked in a rotisserie oven.
dishes.guy_response: Mm. Great crust, but light. That's a good one. Makes the whole sandwich.

dishes.description: Thinly sliced boneless ribeye cooked on a griddle with yellow onions, butter, and salt, then topped with provolone cheese and served on a freshly baked hoagie roll.
dishes.guy_response: You know what the kicker on that is? The butter. The butter is what does that. That should make some folks nervous in Philly. This is what I come here for. This is the best steak sandwich in the whole world.

dishes.description: Meatballs made from ground breadcrumbs, Pecorino Romano cheese, fresh garlic, fresh parsley, onions, celery, eggs, and lean ground beef. Rolled into football shapes, baked at 400 degrees for an hour, and served on a roll with homemade sauce.
dishes.guy_response: The meatball is tender as you can find. Oh, yeah. Great tomato sauce. Mm. Nice job.

restaurants.name: Wario's Beef and Pork
restaurants.guy_intro: Just say Columbus. We're in Columbus, Ohio. This chef is very particular about it. He says you get those kind of cheese steaks in Philly. Here you get Columbus cheese steaks. You got it? OK, I won't say it. Don't mess it up. This is Wario's Beef and Pork.

dishes.description: Rolls made with olive oil, water, yeast, semolina flour, bread flour, sugar, and salt. Mixed, rested, portioned, rolled, seeded, sliced, proofed for 12-18 hours, and baked for 15 minutes at 375 degrees.
A smooth cheese sauce made with milk, four-year aged cheddar, Monterey Jack, sheep's milk pecorino, salt, onion powder, garlic powder, mustard, black pepper, and sodium citrate as a binder.
dishes.guy_response: And the fresh cheese sauce, you can really tell, delicious.

dishes.description: A savory sauce made from super caramelized onions, roasted garlic, pepper, salt, lemon zest, and lemon juice, blended to a paste, then mixed with mayonnaise.
dishes.guy_response: Wario sauce is spot on. The Wario sauce is great.

dishes.description: Ribeye cooked on a flat top with salt and pepper, chopped and spread, topped with Cooper sharp cheese, unsmoked provolone, a 'kiss of whiz', caramelized onions, and Wario sauce, served on a toasted hoagie bun.
dishes.guy_response: I'm bathed in the cheese sauce. It's so good. It is another dimension. The bread is great. Everything from making the roll yourself to making the cheese sauce to studying awareness of great cheesesteakery. Wario sauce is spot on. They're getting a five-star meal on wax paper out of window.

restaurants.name: Gall & Company Malt House
restaurants.owner_chef: Joe Forkin, Jim Lyons, and Matt Yeck
restaurants.guy_intro: I'm here to check out a joint where all the estate buddies came upon a great deal on a pub and they said, hey, why don't we get into the pub and restaurant business? You're gonna dig it. This is the Gall & Company Malt House.

dishes.description: House-made kielbasa (ground pork, paprika, onion powder, black pepper, fresh garlic) baked, sliced, and seared on a flat top. Served with house-made cheese sauce, caramelized onions, and house-cut fries on a hoagie roll.
dishes.guy_response: It's unique. I don't think I've ever had anything like it. It is part ham and cheese. It is part cheesesteak. There's those big flavors that you get in kielbasa. And the uniqueness of how you do it from making it into the loaf, to slicing it, to throwing it on the flat top, kind of caramelizing the sausage. That is a destination sandwich.

restaurants.name: Yumbie
restaurants.guy_intro: So I'm here in Atlanta, Georgia. A couple minutes walk from Piedmont Park. It's kind of like the Central Park of Atlanta. Right next to the Beltline, which is a 22-mile strip where you can bike and walk. And from what I hear about this next restaurant, I'm going to need to hit that. It's a husband and wife restaurant. The husband and wife team, they're doing a mashup between Korean and Mexican. And they were the ones that actually helped start the entire food truck game here in Atlanta. And there's more story than that. This is Yumbie.

dishes.description: Ribeye beef marinated for 72 hours in a bulgogi sauce with onions. Served on a roll with sriracha queso, yummy chili sauce, and cucumber kimchi.
dishes.guy_response: The meat is so tender. The cucumber kimchi is refreshing. Just enough cheese sauce to where it's not heavy and weighted down. Packed with flavor. Delicious. It's juicy, it's tender. The queso on the Philly is fantastic. You get a perfect balance of sweet, spicy. Dude, that's incredible.

restaurants.name: Mom Mom's Kitchen
restaurants.guy_intro: So we're here in Bridesburg, right in the northeast side of Philadelphia, to check out a joint where they're using grandma's recipes to make it the real deal. This is Mom Mom's Kitchen.

dishes.description: Pierogi dough made with sour cream, butter, eggs, flour, and salt. Filled with thinly sliced ribeye and Cooper Sharp American cheese, then boiled, cooled, and seared on a flat top until crispy. Served with caramelized onions and black pepper ketchup.
dishes.guy_response: I've had a lot of pierogis on Triple D, and I've had a lot of pierogis just in regular life. That pierogi dough with the sour cream and the whole barnyard of chickens, it's silky, luxurious, melts in your mouth. Wow. It's absolutely wrecking regular cheesesteaks for me right now. And you guys made this up? Yeah. Don't get me wrong. I like a little sauce. I used to have that little acid, little sweet. Dynamite, dynamite. I mean, this isn't just like, hey, my grandmother was Polish. This is like a legitimate, groundbreaking destination dish.
